conflict.keepCloudBackupDesc: Download cloud version, your changes are saved (use Undo to restore)
conflict.confirmKeepLocal: This will upload your version to the cloud. The previous cloud version can still be recovered from the repository history.
conflict.confirmKeepCloud: This will replace your version with the cloud. Your local changes will be lost and cannot be recovered.

prompt.subtasksMove:
This task has {{count}} subtask(s).

Type:
  "all" - move task and subtasks to backlog
  "only" - move only this task (subtasks become orphans)
  "cancel" - do nothing
prompt.subtasksDelete:
This task has {{count}} subtask(s).

Type:
  "all" - delete task and subtasks
  "only" - delete only this task (subtasks become orphans)
  "cancel" - do nothing
prompt.subtasksRestore:
This task has {{count}} subtask(s) in {{location}}.

Type:
  "all" - restore task and subtasks
  "only" - restore only this task
  "cancel" - do nothing
prompt.subtasksPermanent:
This task has {{count}} subtask(s) in trash.

Type:
  "all" - permanently delete task and subtasks
  "only" - delete only this task
  "cancel" - do nothing

filter.title: Filter by tags
filter.noGroups: No tag groups yet. Create one to organize your tags.

readme.title: Kanbito Data
readme.intro: This repository contains data from [Kanbito](https://github.com/vfmatzkin/kanbito), a personal Kanban board app.
readme.filesBoard: `board.json` - Task metadata (columns, task list, backlog, trash)
readme.filesSettings: `.kanbito.json` - App settings (git sync config, language, username)
readme.restoreIntro: If you need to restore your data to a previous state:
readme.option1Steps:
1. Go to your repository on GitHub
2. Click on "Commits" to see the history
3. Find the commit you want to restore to
4. Click the commit hash, then click "Browse files"
5. Download the files you need (board.json, notes.json, etc.)
6. Replace the files in your Kanbito data folder
readme.option2Code:
# See commit history
git log --oneline

# Restore to a specific commit (replace COMMIT_HASH)
git checkout COMMIT_HASH -- board.json notes.json tasks/ notes/

# Or restore everything to a specific commit
git reset --hard COMMIT_HASH
readme.option3Steps:
1. Go to your repository on GitHub
2. Click on "Commits"
3. Find the commit before the unwanted change
4. Click "..." → "Revert changes in this commit" (creates a new commit)
readme.note: The `.kanbito-backup/` folder (if present) is local only and not synced to GitHub.

examples.clearHint: These are example tasks. Click to start fresh!

1.title: Welcome to Kanbito!
1.comment:
This is a **comment** with Markdown support!

You can reference other tasks like [[T2]] or notes like [[N1]].
1.description:
## Welcome to Kanbito!

This is your personal **Kanban board**. Here's what you can do:

- **Drag & drop** tasks between columns
- **Add tags** to organize and filter tasks
- **Use Markdown** in descriptions and comments
- **Link tasks** with `[[T2]]` or notes with `[[N1]]`

### Try these features:
1. Click the **filter button** (bottom-right) to filter by tags
2. Hover over a task and click **+** to add subtasks
3. Click **⚙️** to enable GitHub sync

See [[T2]] to learn about tag filtering!

2.title: Try filtering your tasks
2.description:
## Learn to filter by tags

Kanbito lets you filter tasks using **tag groups**. Try it now:

1. Click the **filter button** in the bottom-right corner
2. You'll see 3 example groups:
   - **Type**: bug, idea
   - **Area**: work, personal
   - **When**: today, this-week, someday

### How it works:
- Click a tag to filter the board
- Tags in the same group use **OR** logic (show any match)
- Tags across groups use **AND** logic (must match all)

### Customize your groups:
Click "Manage tag groups" to create your own organization system!

> Try filtering by `work` to see only work-related tasks.

1.title: Welcome to Notes
1.content:
# Welcome to Notes

Notes are perfect for documentation, ideas, and longer content.

## Features

- **Hierarchical structure** - Create nested sub-notes
- **Markdown support** - Full formatting
- **Cross-references** - Link to tasks with `[[T1]]`
- **Drag & drop** - Reorganize your notes

## Getting started

Click the **+** button to create new notes. Use the sidebar to navigate.

2.title: Project Ideas
2.content:
# Project Ideas

A place to brainstorm and collect ideas.

## Features to build
- [ ] Dark mode
- [ ] Export to PDF
- [ ] Mobile app

## Research
- Check competitor features
- User feedback themes

---
*Add your own ideas here!*

3.title: Quick Reference
3.content:
# Quick Reference

## Keyboard shortcuts
- `Esc` - Close dialogs
- `Ctrl+Enter` - Save note/comment

## Cross-references
- `[[T5]]` → Links to task 5
- `[[N2]]` → Links to note 2

## Images
Paste images directly with `Ctrl+V`
